title: toxicodendron orientale subsp. hispidum
intro: Toxicodendron orientale subsp. hispidum, also known as the Oriental Poison Ivy, is a plant species belonging to the Anacardiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The resin from the plant's leaves has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may provide relief from pain and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron orientale subsp. hispidum include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to treat various health issues.
